frothy session — open an interactive REPL session over serial

Session opens a richer interactive REPL than connect: it can compile Frothy source on the host when the device advertises host-optional mode, replay a recorded transcript, or record one to NDJSON. Use it when connect is too thin for what you need; for a basic interactive REPL, connect is smaller and faster.

Examples:
  frothy session --port /dev/cu.usbserial-0001
      open an interactive REPL on that port, with host compile when offered

Flags:
  -baud int
    	serial baud rate (default 115200)
  -compiler string
    	C overlay compiler helper
  -dry-run
    	print device lines without opening serial
  -file string
    	load source lines from a file, applying boot definitions last
  -host-compile
    	use host compiler when the device advertises host-optional mode
  -port string
    	serial port, for example /dev/cu.usbmodem101
  -records
    	emit NDJSON session records on stdout
  -replay string
    	replay accepted source from an NDJSON record transcript
  -settle duration
    	delay after opening serial (default 2s)
  -timeout duration
    	serial prompt timeout (default 3s)
  -transcript string
    	write NDJSON session records to a file; requires --records
